questions.question: What is the key difference between forward chaining and backward chaining?
expectedPoints: Forward chaining is data-driven (starts from facts)
Backward chaining is goal-driven (starts from query)
Forward chaining may generate irrelevant facts
Backward chaining is more focused on specific goals

questions.question: Explain the forward chaining algorithm with an example using First-Order Logic rules and facts.
expectedPoints: Start with known facts in knowledge base
Check each rule against available facts
Use unification to match rule premises with facts
Add new conclusions to knowledge base
Repeat until no new inferences can be made
Provide a concrete example with at least 2 rules and 3 facts
